2603.24044.title: MoE-Sieve: Routing-Guided LoRA for Efficient MoE Fine-Tuning
2603.24044.paper_summary: Standard LoRA fine-tuning of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models applies adapters to every expert, yet our profiling shows that per-layer expert routing is highly skewed: a small subset of experts handles most tokens in each layer, while many others are rarely activated ("cold"). We propose MoE-Sieve, a simple routing-guided framework for LoRA fine-tuning, and pair it with a systematic profiling study of expert routing across architectures and tasks. The method is simple: profile routing counts on a small calibration set, select the top-k most-routed experts per layer, and apply LoRA only to those experts. Across two architecturally distinct MoE models and three diverse tasks, tuning only the top 25% routed experts per layer remains competitive with full LoRA, with mean differences within +/-1 percentage point across all conditions. This reduces LoRA trainable parameters by 70-73%, adapter checkpoint size by 71-73%, and wall-clock training time by up to 50%. We also observe a non-monotonic relationship between expert count and seed-to-seed variance, consistent with the hypothesis that adapting cold experts can introduce gradient noise without improving accuracy. Further ablations show that random expert selection at matched budget is about 2.5 percentage points worse, indicating that the routing signal matters, while greedy per-layer budget optimization does not improve over uniform top-k.
2603.24044.paper_summary_zh: 標準的 LoRA 微調 Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) 模型對每個專家應用適配器，但我們的分析顯示每層專家的路由高度偏斜：少數專家處理每層中的大多數標記，而許多其他專家則很少被激活（"冷"）。我們提出 MoE-Sieve，一個簡單的路由引導框架，用於 LoRA 微調，並將其與專家路由在不同架構和任務中的系統性分析研究相結合。這個方法很簡單：在一個小的校準集上分析路由計數，選擇每層中路由數量最多的前 k 名專家，僅對這些專家應用 LoRA。在兩個架構上不同的 MoE 模型和三個不同的任務中，每層僅調整前 25% 的路由專家仍然與完整的 LoRA 競爭，所有條件下的平均差異在 +/-1 個百分點內。這使得 LoRA 可訓練參數減少 70-73%，適配器檢查點大小減少 71-73%，牆鐘訓練時間減少最多 50%。我們還觀察到專家數量與種子到種子變異之間存在非單調關係，這與假設冷專家適應可能引入梯度噪聲而不改善準確性是一致的。進一步的消融實驗顯示，在匹配預算的隨機專家選擇大約差 2.5 個百分點，這表明路由信號是重要的，而貪婪的每層預算優化並未優於均勻的前 k 名選擇。
2603.24044.author: Andrea Manzoni et.al.
